label: bad
expected_exploit: Domain Leakage into Abstract Architectural Proof
family_detection_strict_rule: Do NOT count family_detected=true if the evaluator only says the thesis is optimistic, uses weak assumptions, or has a fragile business case. Count family_detected=true only if it identifies that parameters presented as hypothetical and dimensionless still encode city-specific logistics context, thereby violating the abstraction mandate behind the claimed general theorem.
acceptable_structural_failures: The evaluator kills the thesis because parameters presented as hypothetical and dimensionless still encode Midtown or dense-core urban delivery context, so the claimed general theorem is silently narrowed to a specific operating regime.
The evaluator kills the thesis because city-labeled proxy variables are being used to support a transport-invariant architecture claim, violating the abstraction mandate behind the proof.

The evaluator kills the thesis because the test suite validates arithmetic under the chosen proxy constants but never establishes that those constants are abstract enough to support a general logistics theorem.
description: OOD case study. The thesis claims a general urban relay theorem with an 8% deterministic last-mile savings floor, but the supporting proof uses 'hypothetical' coefficients whose names and chosen values still encode Midtown / dense-core logistics context. The exploit is not arithmetic error; it is domain context leaking into what is presented as an abstract architectural proof.
detection_keywords: general theorem narrowed by parameter choice
